entries.d: When there is a lot of white frozen water on the ground

entries.d: When the sky is clear and bright with no clouds

entries.d: When the air is thick and grey and you cannot see far

entries.d: A very high temperature that makes you feel warm

entries.d: To keep going and not stop

entries.d: A little bit cold, but not too much

entries.d: A unit to measure how warm or cold it is

entries.d: Having no water or rain

entries.d: A set of numbers used to measure something
entries.s: On a _____ of one to ten, how much do you like maths?

entries.d: light and warmth from the sun
entries.s: We had lovely warm _____ all day with a blue sky, so we stayed outside.

entries.d: How warm or cold something is
entries.s: The _____ dropped to zero degrees last night and water froze in the puddles.

entries.d: When bad sky conditions go away and it gets nice
entries.s: Don't worry — the rain will _____ by lunchtime.

entries.d: A thick grey cloud near the ground that makes it hard to see
entries.s: I could not see the road because of the thick white _____ in the early morning.

entries.d: A report that tells you what the sky and air will be like
entries.s: I checked the weather _____ on TV for tomorrow and it said it would rain all afternoon.

entries.d: The feeling that something good will happen
entries.s: There is _____ that the weather will get better soon.

entries.d: What things will probably be like in the future
entries.s: The weather _____ for the next few weeks shows warm and dry conditions ahead.

entries.d: light conversation about the weather or weekend plans
entries.s: British people love making _____ about the weather. They have short, friendly chats everywhere.

entries.d: Wide, heavy, or closely packed together
entries.s: She wore a _____ winter coat made of wool to stay warm in the freezing cold.

entries.d: In the direction of something
entries.s: Walk _____ the park -- in its direction -- and you will find the shop.

entries.d: A friendly thing you say when someone is leaving
entries.s: See you tomorrow. _____ Enjoy your time until then.

entries.d: To check that something is right or done
entries.s: _____ you close the windows carefully before you leave. Don't forget — it might rain.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with MAKE?

entries.d: To go up, to get higher

entries.d: Full of light, easy to see

entries.d: To give off light

entries.d: The normal or middle amount

entries.d: Lower than something, under

entries.d: Not too hot and not too cold, gentle

entries.d: A tool with two lenses you look through to see things far away
entries.s: I used my _____ to watch the birds on the lake.

entries.d: The job or work someone does for many years

entries.d: To get money by working
entries.s: My brother _____ 50 euros a week by helping at a bakery after school — the baker pays him.

entries.d: To like something very, very much
entries.s: My sister is _____ horses. She rides every day.
